Sharp moral dichotomies: 'good guys and bad guys,' 'good insiders vs. evil enemy'
Temporal oscillation without integration: 'I hate X... actually I love X' (stated as separate truths)
Categorical assignments: 'You understand, of course, that this is not what I really think'
Compartmentalized opposing affects: 'my love for him is gone' followed by '(I still love him as I used to)'
Mythological binaries: 'God versus the devil,' 'cowboys versus Indians,' 'free world against terrorists'
Staff division language: 'divides the staff into good guys and bad guys'
Assignment of valences: 'assigning good and bad valences to everything'
Manichean visions expressed as absolute truths
Alternating idealization and devaluation statements about the same object
narrative_patterns: Assigns all positive qualities to one person/group and all negative qualities to another, maintaining both positions simultaneously
Oscillates between contradictory emotional stances toward the same object without synthesizing them
Organizes complex relational experiences into simplified hero/villain narratives
Maintains 'double-entry bookkeeping' of affects—anger and love exist toward one person but never blend
Creates invasive interpersonal pressure on others to conform to the good/bad categorization
Divides surrounding people (staff, family, friends) into opposing camps that mirror the internal split

differentiation.vs_Reaction_Formation: In reaction formation the user is oblivious to ambivalence and truly treats black as white, while in splitting a gray world is separated into all black and all white with the individual remaining consciously divided against themselves.
differentiation.vs_Dissociation: Dissociation involves alterations in consciousness where experiences are held separately and never integrated, whereas splitting involves the segregation of what has been or could be integrated into opposing affective categories without amnesia or switches in consciousness.
differentiation.vs_Projective_Identification: Projective identification involves ejecting unwanted aspects while inducing them in others and maintaining empathy with what is projected, whereas splitting assigns good and bad valences to different objects/people without the interpersonal induction or need to maintain connection with the disavowed content.

Splitting.literary_cues: Splitting produces narrative architecture built on unresolvable antithesis—the syntax itself refuses synthesis, moving between idealization and denigration without transitional modulation or subordinate clauses that might acknowledge complexity. The voice oscillates between hagiography and demonization, with imagerial clusters that never contaminate one another; angels and demons occupy adjacent stanzas but never share metaphoric space. Temporal structure reflects affective compartmentalization: past and present versions of the same object are described as if they were different entities, and the speaker's stance shifts categorically rather than evolving, creating a fragmented autobiography in which contradictory truths coexist without irony or dialectical tension.

Sublimation.name: Sublimation
Sublimation.definition: The indirect or attenuated expression of biologically based impulses and infantile conflicts through socially valuable, creative, or beneficial activity without adverse consequences or marked loss of pleasure. Primitive urges are channeled and harnessed into constructive forms rather than suppressed or dissipated, maintaining the intensity of feeling while transforming its mode of expression into work, art, relationships, or play with real consequences.
linguistic_signals: "I channel [impulse] into [creative/productive activity]"
References to finding outlet or use in other fields
"transforms [primitive urge] through [socially valuable form]"
Language of artistic expression, performance, or creative production
Description of work as passionate, engrossing, or transporting
"[Activity] allows me to express [feeling] without..."
References to catharsis through creative means
Blending of opposing feelings (love/hate, desire/constraint) in productive activity
Language suggesting intensity is maintained but redirected rather than dampened
References to models, incentives, or identification with creative figures
narrative_patterns: Describes intense engagement in work, art, or socially valued activity that clearly relates to underlying conflicts
Presents creative or productive outlets as naturally arising solutions rather than forced compromises
Maintains emotional authenticity and pleasure while discussing transformed expression of impulses
Links current activities to primitive desires or conflicts without shame or complete disguise
Demonstrates real consequences and tangible products from psychological transformation
Shows identification with mentors or models who exemplify the sublimated activity

differentiation.vs_displacement: Sublimation produces socially valuable outcomes and maintains more emotional intensity with real consequences, whereas displacement merely redirects feelings toward safer targets without creative transformation or social contribution; writing New Yorker poetry (sublimation) versus subway graffiti (displacement) may differ more in education than maturity.
differentiation.vs_reaction_formation: Sublimation channels and harnesses affects while allowing attenuated expression and pleasure, whereas reaction formation dams or completely reverses impulses, producing opposite behaviors without creative outlet or authentic emotional satisfaction.
differentiation.vs_humor: Sublimation is the broader category of creative transformation of impulses into socially valuable forms, while humor is a specific subtype that reconciles paradox and transforms sadomasochism into gentle self-mockery or clowning; both achieve catharsis but humor specifically uses comic reconciliation of opposites.

Sublimation.literary_cues: Sublimation produces writing marked by sustained passionate engagement with subject matter that simultaneously reveals and transforms primitive material—the voice maintains emotional authenticity while achieving aesthetic or intellectual distance through formal craft. Syntax tends toward complex integration rather than splitting, weaving together conflicting affects into unified creative statements. The stance is neither detached nor raw but alchemical, as if heat and pressure have produced a new compound from base elements without loss of energy.

Suppression.name: Suppression
Suppression.definition: A semiconscious decision to postpone—rather than eliminate—attention to a conscious impulse or conflict, maintaining awareness of all components (idea, affect, object) while waiting a finite time period. Unlike repression, the user remembers the conflict and does not lose cherished goals; unlike dissociation, gratification is genuinely deferred rather than optimistically assumed. The capacity requires delicate balance comparable to tightrope walking—voluntary yet requiring ego coordination that cannot simply be willed.
linguistic_signals: "I'll think about it tomorrow" (with actual intention to do so)
"I'm keeping [desire/worry] in mind while waiting"
"Progressing anxiously and slowly"
"Not now, but later" (with finite timeframe)
"Uncomplainingly waiting"
"The glass is half full" (realistic acknowledgment)
"Saving now for next summer"
"Postponing [specific impulse] until [concrete future event]"
Minimizes without ignoring: "I see the problem, but I'm setting it aside for now"
"Keeping both [attachment] and [limits] in mind"
narrative_patterns: Acknowledges conflict explicitly before stating intention to delay addressing it
Describes incremental progression through stages (handholding → kissing → petting) with temporal markers
Balances opposing forces in consciousness without resolution: notes both desire and constraint simultaneously
Frames waiting as active choice with endpoint rather than avoidance
Shows awareness of consequences and realistic planning for future action
Maintains connection to the conflicted object/person throughout the narrative

differentiation.vs_repression: Suppression retains conscious memory of the conflict and all its components, whereas repression involves unconscious inhibition to the point of losing awareness and cherished goals, often accompanied by symbolic behavior revealing what has been forgotten.
differentiation.vs_dissociation: Suppression genuinely postpones gratification with realistic planning (saves now for later travel), while dissociation acts optimistically without real deferral (flies now assuming ability to pay later); the stoic notes the glass is half full, Pollyanna leaves her umbrella at home despite clouds.
differentiation.vs_isolation: Suppression keeps idea, affect, and object together in partial consciousness as an integrated whole, while isolation splits cognitive awareness from emotional experience, severing the connection between thought and feeling.

Suppression.literary_cues: The voice of suppression balances on a syntactic tightrope, holding tension between opposing forces in compound constructions ("both... and...") without collapse into resolution. Temporal markers pervade the discourse—"tomorrow," "next summer," "finite time"—anchoring deferral in concrete futurity rather than indefinite postponement. The stance is stoic yet aware, acknowledging difficulty ("anxiously," "uncomplainingly") while maintaining narrative forward motion through incremental stages, each step named and owned rather than glossed or forgotten.

Undoing.name: Undoing
Undoing.definition: The unconscious effort to counterbalance guilt or shame through a compensatory attitude or behavior that magically erases the transgressive thought, wish, or deed. The individual is unaware of the underlying affect being expiated and implicitly treats hostile or forbidden thoughts as tantamount to dangerous deeds, employing omnipotent fantasies that require ritualistic neutralization. This defense involves expressing a wish or affect and then ritually denying or atoning for it through symbolic reversals.
linguistic_signals: Knocking on wood after boasting about health or good fortune
"I guess they were really for your grave" (recognizing gifts as atonement)
"Step on a crack, break your mother's back" (verbalized hostility stripped of affect)
Expressions of remorse for sins "committed only in thought"
References to making amends or compensating without conscious acknowledgment of transgression
Magical or superstitious phrasing accompanying assertions
Ritual verbal formulations that counterbalance preceding statements
Language of atonement, expiation, or balancing accounts
narrative_patterns: Bringing gifts or performing kind acts after unexpressed anger, with the connection remaining unconscious
Engaging in religious rituals or moral crusades to atone for past sins or failures, real or imagined
Childhood games and rituals designed to ward off feared consequences of hostile wishes (avoiding sidewalk cracks)
Dedicating oneself to causes that reverse earlier inadvertent transgressions (lifetime altruism following childhood insult)
Performing compensatory behaviors immediately after assertive or aggressive expressions
Ritualistic actions that follow forbidden thoughts without conscious awareness of the connection
example_dsq_items: Items assessing undoing appear in the Neurotic defense category (4 items) across DSQ studies
No specific item content was quoted in the provided passages

differentiation.vs_isolation_of_affect: Undoing attempts to magically reverse or neutralize a thought/act through compensatory behavior, whereas isolation removes the emotional charge from the thought while retaining cognitive awareness; undoing is behavioral/ritualistic atonement, isolation is cognitive-affective disconnection.
differentiation.vs_reaction_formation: Undoing seeks to cancel out or counterbalance a forbidden impulse after it has been expressed (temporal sequence: transgression then atonement), while reaction formation prevents the impulse from emerging by replacing it with its opposite continuously; undoing is episodic compensation, reaction formation is sustained transformation.
differentiation.vs_rationalization: Undoing employs magical thinking and ritualistic behavior to negate guilt-inducing acts or thoughts, whereas rationalization uses logical-sounding explanations to justify behavior; undoing treats thoughts as dangerous deeds requiring symbolic reversal, rationalization reframes them as acceptable through pseudo-logic.

Undoing.literary_cues: Undoing manifests in narrative voice through obsessive symmetries and compulsive balancing gestures—a speaker may pivot abruptly from assertion to superstitious qualification, creating couplets of claim and retraction. The syntax often contains ritual insertions (knocking-wood phrases) that function as verbal talismans. Imagery gravitates toward ledgers, graves covered with flowers, and childhood sidewalk games where each step carries cosmic weight, revealing an omnipotent universe where thoughts wound and gestures heal.
